title: 10 best herbal teas for hair fall
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hair fall are infusions made from various plants and herbs, which are believed to help reduce hair fall and promote hair growth. These teas are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that nourish the scalp and hair follicles, improving hair health and reducing breakage. Examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for hair fall include nettle, rosemary, horsetail, and hibiscus tea. By consuming these teas regularly, individuals may experience a decrease in hair fall, an improvement in hair thickness and texture, and an overall enhancement in their appearance and self-confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that promote hair growth. These properties help improve blood circulation in the scalp, which in turn stimulates hair follicles and encourages hair growth. Additionally, rosemary tea contains nutrients such as iron, calcium, and vitamin B that are essential for healthy hair growth. By drinking rosemary tea regularly, you can provide your body with the necessary nutrients to support strong and thick hair, reducing hair fall and promoting overall hair health.
remedy_properties: Promotes hair growth: Rosemary tea stimulates hair growth by increasing blood circulation in the scalp, which helps to deliver essential nutrients to hair follicles. This property is attributed to its active constituents, such as carnosol and ursolic acid.

Prevents premature graying: Rosemary tea contains antioxidants that protect hair follicles from oxidative stress, which can cause premature graying and hair fall. The antioxidant properties come from rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid present in rosemary.

Reduces scalp inflammation: Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe an irritated scalp, reducing inflammation and dandruff, which can lead to hair fall. The anti-inflammatory effects are due to the presence of compounds like carnosol and carnosic acid.

Strengthens hair: Rosemary tea contains nutrients that help to strengthen hair, reducing breakage and promoting healthy hair growth. The strengthening properties can be attributed to the presence of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants in rosemary.

Balances hormones: Rosemary tea has been shown to balance hormones, particularly androgens, which can contribute to hair fall. By maintaining hormonal balance, rosemary tea helps promote healthy hair growth and reduces hair fall. The hormone-balancing effects are attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other polyphenolic compounds in rosemary.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and let it cool down for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the rosemary leaves.
Drink the rosemary tea daily, preferably before or after meals, for best results in reducing hair fall.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary leaves are commonly used to make medicinal teas for hair fall due to their potential to improve scalp health and promote hair growth.
Flowers: Packed with essential oils and antioxidants, rosemary flowers can be used in medicinal teas for hair fall to nourish the scalp, improve circulation, and enhance hair thickness.

Seeds: Containing high levels of fatty acids and antioxidants, rosemary seeds are used in medicinal teas for hair fall to strengthen hair follicles, moisturize the scalp, and support overall hair health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with hair fall because it contains powerful antioxidants that can help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals. The tea is also rich in anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, which can lead to hair fall. Additionally, lavender tea has been shown to improve circulation, which can help to promote hair growth and reduce hair fall. The tea can be consumed internally or used as a rinse to directly target the scalp and hair. Regular use of lavender tea can help to strengthen the hair, improve its overall health, and reduce hair fall.
remedy_properties: Promotes Hair Growth: English lavender tea stimulates hair growth by increasing the number of hair follicles and improving blood circulation in the scalp, due to its active constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce scalp inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to hair fall, thanks to the presence of compounds like tannins and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Lavender tea has antimicrobial properties that help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can lead to hair loss, attributed to its essential oils and polyphenols.

Stress Reduction: English lavender tea has stress-relieving properties that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can indirectly contribute to hair fall reduction, as stress is a known trigger for hair loss, due to the calming effects of linalool and linalyl acetate.
Nutrient-rich: The tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as A, C, and calcium, which support hair growth and strength, contributing to overall hair health and reducing hair fall.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the tea into your scalp and leave it on for 20 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers help promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
Leaves: Contain essential oils that can improve blood circulation in the scalp, reducing hair fall.
Stems: High in nutrients that can strengthen hair follicles, preventing hair fall and promoting hair growth.
Seeds: Full of fatty acids that can nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair fall.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in antioxidants and essential oils that stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing shedding. The herb's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also help to maintain a healthy scalp by soothing irritation and combating dandruff or other fungal infections. Furthermore, thyme is a natural source of vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, vitamin C, and iron, which are crucial for hair strength and growth. Regularly consuming thyme tea or using it as a hair rinse can contribute to thicker, fuller hair and lessen the likelihood of excessive hair fall.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce scalp inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to hair fall. This is due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that can help maintain a healthy scalp environment, free from harmful bacteria and fungi that can cause hair fall. The active constituents responsible for this property include thymol and carvacrol.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the scalp and hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy hair growth. The primary antioxidants in thyme tea are rosmarinic acid and luteolin.

Nutrient-rich: Thyme tea is rich in essential nutrients like vitamins A and C, iron, and manganese, which are vital for hair growth and preventing hair fall. These nutrients help strengthen hair follicles and promote circulation, ensuring that hair receives the necessary nutrients for growth.

Dihydrotestosterone: Thyme tea has been shown to inhibit the production of DHT, a hormone that contributes to hair fall. This property is attributed to the presence of ursolic acid and oleanolic acid in thyme tea.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea into a container and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Apply the thyme tea to your scalp and hair, massaging gently for 5 minutes.
Leave the tea on your hair for 15 minutes, then rinse it out thoroughly with warm water.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, making them an excellent choice for promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain beneficial compounds that can improve blood circulation in the scalp, which can help strengthen hair follicles and reduce hair fall.
Stems: Thyme stems contain important nutrients that can help nourish the scalp and hair, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair fall.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in antioxidants and vitamins that promote hair growth and reduce hair loss. The anti-inflammatory properties of sage tea can help soothe an irritated scalp, reducing dandruff and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, sage tea contains beta-sistosterol, a plant compound that has been shown to block the production of DHT, a hormone linked to hair loss. By strengthening hair follicles and improving scalp health, sage tea can help reduce hair fall and promote thicker, fuller hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce scalp inflammation, which can contribute to hair fall. This is due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in sage tea helps protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth. This is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Anti-androgenic: Sage tea has anti-androgenic properties that can help block the production of DHT, a hormone responsible for hair loss in both men and women. This is due to the presence of safficinolide and its ability to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase.

Circulation-boosting: Sage tea helps improve blood circulation in the scalp, ensuring that nutrients reach hair follicles and promote hair growth. This is due to the presence of essential oils like cineole and borneol.

Dandruff-reducing: Sage tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties help reduce dandruff, which can clog hair follicles and lead to hair fall. This is due to the presence of compounds like thujone and camphor.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried sage leaves to the boiling water, then let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Apply the sage tea to your scalp and damp hair, massaging it gently for 5 minutes.
Leave the tea on your hair for 20 minutes, then rinse it out thoroughly with warm water.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant are high in nutrients, including vitamins A, C, and E, which can help to strengthen hair follicles and reduce hair fall.
Flowers: The flowers of the sage plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to improve blood circulation in the scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Seeds: The seeds of the sage plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help to nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth, reducing hair fall.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in nutrients, including vitamins A, C, and B, as well as minerals like zinc, magnesium, and iron. These nutrients promote hair growth and prevent hair fall by strengthening hair follicles and improving blood circulation in the scalp. Additionally, stinging nettle tea contains antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can also contribute to hair loss. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe an irritated scalp and reduce dandruff, which can further promote healthy hair growth. Overall, stinging nettle tea is a natural and effective remedy for preventing hair fall and promoting thicker, fuller hair.
remedy_properties: Nutrient-rich: Stinging nettle tea is packed with vitamins A, C, and D, as well as minerals like calcium, magnesium, and iron, which are essential for hair growth and reducing hair fall.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of polyphenolic compounds and flavonoids, can help soothe an irritated scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.

DHT inhibition: Stinging nettle contains compounds that can inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone responsible for male pattern baldness, thereby reducing hair fall.
Anti-androgenic: The anti-androgenic properties of stinging nettle tea, attributed to lignans and sterols, can help block the action of androgens, hormones that can contribute to hair loss.
Circulation improvement: Stinging nettle tea can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, providing essential nutrients to hair follicles and promoting hair growth, thereby reducing hair fall.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried stinging nettle leaves and add to a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour over the nettle leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Apply the tea to your scalp and hair, massaging it in gently, and let it sit for 15-20 minutes before rinsing out.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Nettle leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals that can help promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
Roots: Nettle roots contain compounds that can help block the production of hormones responsible for hair loss, making it an effective ingredient for hair fall teas.
Seeds: Nettle seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids and antioxidants that can help nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Flowers: Nettle flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair fall.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in silica and other minerals that are essential for hair growth. Silica helps to strengthen hair follicles, making them less likely to fall out. It can also promote the growth of new hair by stimulating blood circulation in the scalp. Additionally, horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce damage to the hair and scalp, further promoting healthy hair growth. Drinking horsetail tea regularly can help to provide the nutrients needed to support strong, healthy hair and reduce hair fall.
remedy_properties: High in silica: Horsetail tea is rich in silica, a mineral that helps strengthen hair follicles and promotes hair growth, reducing hair fall.
Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce scalp inflammation and improve hair health, thereby reducing hair fall.
Antioxidant properties: Horsetail tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, reducing hair fall.
Promotes blood circulation: Horsetail tea helps improve blood circulation in the scalp, ensuring that nutrients reach the hair follicles, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
Rich in minerals: Horsetail tea is rich in minerals such as potassium, magnesium, and calcium, which are essential for healthy hair growth and can help reduce hair fall.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb, simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, cover and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, discard the solid remains.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, 1-2 times a day, consistently for several months for best results.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and other minerals, making them useful for promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain high levels of silica, which can help strengthen hair and reduce breakage, making it an ideal part for medicinal teas for hair fall.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are a source of various nutrients, including flavonoids and saponins, which can help improve hair health and reduce hair fall when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek tea helps with hair fall because it contains proteins, nicotinic acid, and large amounts of lecithin, which are known to strengthen hair follicles and promote hair growth. The tea is also rich in iron, calcium, and vitamin C, which are essential for healthy hair. Furthermore, fenugreek has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry and itchy scalp, reducing dandruff and promoting hair growth. Regular consumption of fenugreek tea can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, nourishing the hair follicles and reducing hair fall. Additionally, the tea can help strengthen the hair shaft, preventing breakage and promoting longer, thicker hair.
remedy_properties: Promotes Hair Growth: Fenugreek tea stimulates hair growth by nourishing the hair follicles with its rich protein and nicotinic acid content.
Reduces Inflammation: The anti-inflammatory properties of fenugreek tea, due to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, can help reduce scalp inflammation, a potential cause of hair fall.
Strengthens Hair: The high content of lecithin in fenugreek tea strengthens hair by providing it with a protective layer that locks in moisture, reducing breakage and hair fall.
Enhances Blood Circulation: Fenugreek tea improves blood flow to the scalp, ensuring that nutrients reach the hair roots, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
Prevents Hair Loss: The high content of antioxidants in fenugreek tea, such as vitamins C and E, helps fight free radicals, preventing oxidative stress and subsequent hair loss.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried fenugreek seeds, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Filter - Strain the tea to remove the fenugreek seeds, discarding the solids.
Customize - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Mix - Combine 1-2 teaspoons of fresh aloe vera gel into the tea and stir well.
Apply - Massage the tea into your scalp and leave it on for 30 minutes before rinsing with warm water.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are commonly used in medicinal teas for hair fall due to their high content of proteins and nicotinic acid, which are known to promote hair growth and nourish the scalp.
Leaves: Fenugreek leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat hair loss, as they contain compounds that can strengthen hair follicles and stimulate hair growth.
Flowers: Fenugreek flowers are rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, which can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair fall.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in antioxidants and nutrients that promote hair growth and reduce shedding. The tea contains essential oils, such as thymol and carvacrol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp and improve overall scalp health. Oregano tea is also a natural source of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as zinc and iron, which are essential for hair growth and preventing hair fall. Drinking oregano tea or using it as a rinse can help strengthen hair follicles, improve circulation to the scalp, and stimulate the growth of thicker, fuller hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Oregano tea is rich in antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and thymol, which help protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, reducing hair fall.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in oregano tea, including beta-caryophyllin and terpinene, can help reduce scalp inflammation and irritation, promoting healthy hair growth and minimizing hair fall.

Antimicrobial properties: Oregano tea has strong antimicrobial properties due to its content of carvacrol and thymol, which can help maintain a clean and healthy scalp by fighting off fungal and bacterial infections that may cause hair fall.

Nutrient-rich: Oregano tea is a good source of essential vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin E, vitamin C, and zinc, that contribute to hair strength, growth, and overall hair health, reducing hair fall.

DHT-inhibiting properties: Some studies suggest that oregano tea may help block the production of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to hair loss, by inhibiting the activity of the 5-alpha-reductase enzyme, thus promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove oregano leaves.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the tea into your scalp and leave it on for 30 minutes before washing your hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, leaves are often used in teas to help promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, oregano flowers can help improve scalp health and reduce hair fall.
Seeds: High in nutrients, oregano seeds can help strengthen hair follicles and reduce hair fall when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with hair fall because it contains nutrients and antioxidants that promote hair growth and improve scalp health. The menthol present in peppermint tea increases blood circulation in the scalp, which encourages hair growth and reduces hair fall. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce dandruff, which can also contribute to hair fall. Drinking peppermint tea can also help reduce stress, which is a common cause of hair fall. Overall, peppermint tea can be a natural and effective remedy to prevent hair fall and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce scalp inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to hair fall. The active constituents responsible for this property include menthol and flavonoids.

Promotes hair growth: Peppermint tea stimulates hair growth by increasing the number of follicles, promoting blood circulation in the scalp, and prolonging the anagen phase of the hair growth cycle. The main active constituent responsible for this property is menthol.

Rich in antioxidants: Peppermint tea contains high levels of antioxidants that protect the hair follicles from free radical damage, promoting healthy hair growth. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Soothes itchy scalp: Peppermint tea has a calming effect on the scalp, helping to alleviate itchiness, dryness, and dandruff, all of which can contribute to hair fall. The active constituents responsible for this property include menthol and various flavonoids.

Strengthens hair: Peppermint tea contains essential nutrients that strengthen the hair shaft, reducing breakage and promoting hair growth. The active constituents responsible for this property include vitamin A, vitamin C, and various minerals.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the mixture to steep for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the peppermint leaves, pouring the liquid into a clean container.
Let the peppermint tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the cooled peppermint tea into your scalp, leaving it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, menthol, and rosmarinic acid, peppermint leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for hair fall due to their ability to promote hair growth and reduce hair fall by improving blood circulation in the scalp.

Flowers: High in flavonoids and volatile oils, peppermint flowers are used in medicinal teas for hair fall as they help in reducing inflammation and soothing an itchy scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.

Seeds: Peppermint seeds are a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, which are essential for maintaining healthy hair and reducing hair fall. They are often used in medicinal teas to nourish the scalp and promote hair growth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with hair fall because it is rich in flavonoids, plant compounds known for their antioxidant properties. These antioxidants help protect the scalp and hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair loss. Additionally, yarrow tea contains nutrients like potassium, calcium, and magnesium that promote healthy hair growth. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce scalp inflammation, a common cause of hair fall. Furthermore, yarrow tea stimulates blood flow to the scalp, ensuring that hair follicles receive the necessary nutrients for growth and preventing excessive shedding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, can help reduce scalp inflammation, promoting hair growth and minimizing hair fall.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, due to tannins and volatile oils, can help tighten hair follicles and reduce excess sebum, thereby preventing hair fall caused by oily scalp.
Antioxidant: Yarrow tea's antioxidant capacity, provided by phenolic acids and flavonoids, can protect hair follicles from oxidative stress, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing hair fall.

Circulation-enhancing: Yarrow tea's ability to improve blood circulation, thanks to compounds like lactones and essential oils, can help increase nutrient supply to hair follicles, supporting hair growth and minimizing hair fall.

Anti-androgenic: Yarrow tea's anti-androgenic properties, attributed to lactones like asteraceae, can help block the conversion of testosterone to DHT, a hormone responsible for pattern hair loss, ultimately reducing hair fall.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried yarrow herb and place it in a pot.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb pieces, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the cooled yarrow tea into your scalp and leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing it out with water.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots are rich in nutrients and have been used to help strengthen hair follicles, promoting hair growth.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves contain essential oils that can help stimulate blood circulation in the scalp, which may reduce hair fall.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp, reducing hair fall caused by inflammation.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are a great source of fatty acids that can help nourish the scalp and hair, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hair fall with herbal teas is by using them as a natural rinse after shampooing. Herbal teas such as green, nettle, and rosemary teas are known to promote hair growth and reduce shedding. These teas contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to improve scalp health and strengthen hair follicles. To prepare, steep the tea bags in hot water for 15-20 minutes, let it cool down and then massage it onto your scalp. Leave it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out. Regular use of herbal teas as a hair rinse can help to reduce hair fall and promote thicker, fuller hair.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hair fall that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff, scalp inflammation, and dull hair. Herbal teas like nettle, burdock, and horsetail are rich in vitamins and minerals that promote hair growth and improve hair health. Nettle tea is known to block the hormone DHT, which causes hair loss, while burdock tea stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth. Horsetail tea is rich in silica, which strengthens hair and improves its elasticity. Regular consumption of these herbal teas can help improve hair health and reduce hair fall. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment.
